🇺🇸 🇧🇷
online

~/matoanselmo

Mateus Anselmo · computer science student

I run servers at home for fun, break them on purpose, and write down what I learn. Mostly systems, networking, and the small tools in between.

// about

I'm a computer science student who got into self-hosting and never quite looked back. Most of what I actually know I learned by running matoserver — an old ThinkCentre under my desk — into the ground and bringing it back up.

I like systems that are small, legible, and mine. I'd rather understand ten lines than depend on a thousand. This whole site is driven by a single config file, which feels about right.

// projects
this site config-driven

The page you're reading. Every word, project, and language lives in one tidy config object — change the data, the layout follows. No CMS, no clutter.

config-driven i18n no-cms
matoserver homelab

My personal homelab. Debian + Docker, a dozen containers living behind a single dashboard. The thing I learn the most from.

Debian Docker self-hosted
ender-3-klipper 3d printing

Ender 3 V3 SE re-flashed with Klipper. Custom printer.cfg, input shaping, pressure advance, and a Mainsail UI I check far too often.

Klipper Mainsail G-code
// now

What I'm actually doing at the moment —

  • →Migrating matoserver to a quieter, lower-power mini-PC.
  • →Learning Rust by rewriting a few small CLI tools.
  • →Reading about distributed systems, slowly.
updated June 2026
~/github.com/matoanselmo built from a config file
